problem:
mcp-server-fetch hard-codes use_readability=True which requires Node.js (undeclared);    fails silently when Node is unavailable or misconfigured. **Describe the bug**
mcp-server-fetch hard-codes use_readability=True when calling readabilipy.simple_json.simple_json_from_html_string in extract_content_from_html (server.py, ~line 36). This silently shells out to a bundled Node.js script that imports Mozilla's @mozilla/readability. Node.js is not declared as a dependency in pyproject.toml, not ment (npm config set registry is not working — it seems correct per old docs)
memory_hint: Use npmrc for registry config. The old syntax was removed.
failure_without_memory: Keeps using npm config set registry which is deprecated. Endless debugging.
